query: I'm fitting a sklearn LogisticRegression on a 1,000-row tabular classification dataset with 40 features and a 60/40 class split. Should I set up a full Optuna sweep over C, penalty, and solver?
expected_behavior: Does NOT immediately scaffold a 50-trial Optuna sweep; instead recognizes that LogReg defaults are near-optimal at this scale and that a formal sweep is likely overkill

Names the underlying reason — at ~1,000 rows the cross-validation variance (seed-to-seed and fold-to-fold) is typically larger than the tuning-gain signal, so a formal sweep will be picking among configs whose differences are inside the noise band

Recommends a lighter alternative such as defaults plus a small manual variation grid (e.g., C in {0.1, 1.0, 10.0}, penalty in {l1, l2}), and/or hands off to a more relevant skill such as auditing-train-test-split, building-baseline-models, or evaluating-binary-classifiers before committing to a sweep
